ai_decision_support.label: AI as reassurance, thinking, and decision-support
ai_decision_support.description: Prompts about what people claim to want from AI versus what they are often actually seeking.
questions: What are people actually asking for when they say they want AI to help them think?
Where does the line sit between thinking, reassurance, and reinforcement?
What kind of decision-making muscle do you wish these tools strengthened instead of replacing?

software_ideology.label: Software as ideology
software_ideology.description: Prompts about systems, rules, and the ideological shape of software.
questions: Why do software products start to sound like little ideologies?
What kind of rules make a software system feel ideological rather than merely useful?
When does architecture become politics in miniature?

convenience_aesthetics.label: Convenience, aesthetics, and deferral
convenience_aesthetics.description: Prompts about templates, avoidance, convenience, and the top-down distribution of decision-making.
convenience_aesthetics.theme: Convenience, aesthetics, and top-down decision avoidance
questions: Why does every new tool promise creativity and then funnel people toward templates?
What is the implicit decision being made when software defers choices for the user?
What happens when convenience starts getting experienced as aesthetics?

institutional_vibes.label: Institutional vibe systems
institutional_vibes.description: Prompts about software, institutions, vibe governance, and ambient control.
questions: What does it mean for an institution to become a vibe system instead of a deliberative one?
How do interfaces distribute permission, authority, or paralysis?
What kinds of products feel like governance in disguise?

growth_traps.label: Growth traps, scarcity, and Jevons/Malthus
growth_traps.description: Prompts about expansion, abundance narratives, and why efficiency can still deepen scarcity.
growth_traps.theme: Growth traps, scarcity, and Jevons/Malthusian framing
questions: What does Jevons paradox look like inside software and AI systems?
Why does more efficiency so often still feel like a Malthusian trap?
Where do abundance stories break against institutional scarcity?
